title: oxalis flava
intro: Oxalis flava, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anthelmintic and antiseptic properties, which help to treat worm infestations and skin infections. The leaves and flowers of the plant have been used to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as a diuretic, and serving as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Oxalis flava can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the leaves and flowers being dried and used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
